display_information.name: Slackaki
display_information.description: Iron Man's Jarvis for coding agents, inside Slack.
display_information.long_description: Slackaki connects Slack to OpenCode, a coding agent that runs on your machine. Each channel is linked to a project directory; messages in that channel open a thread and start an AI session that can read, edit, and run code.

slash_commands.description: Start a new session with an initial prompt
Run a session in the background; DM when done or input needed
Schedule a recurring task (natural language or cron)
List scheduled tasks in this channel
Remove a scheduled task by id
Resume a previous session
Stop the current running session
Link a channel to an existing OpenCode project
Create a new project folder and start a session
Create a git worktree and start a session
Merge worktree branch into the default branch
Change the AI model for this channel or session
Change the agent for this channel or session
Generate a public URL to share the current session
Fork the session from a previous message
Queue a message to send after the current response finishes
Clear all queued messages in this thread
Undo the last assistant message (revert file changes)
Redo the last undone message
Share your screen via VNC tunnel (auto-stops after 1h)
Stop screen sharing
Upgrade Slackaki to latest and restart the bridge

shortcuts.name: Fork from here
shortcuts.description: Start a new session branched from this message

shortcuts.name: Include in session
shortcuts.description: Add this message as extra context to the current session

shortcuts.name: New Slackaki session
shortcuts.description: Pick a project and start a new session
